_source: King §12, Appendix A p.489: words/classes resistant to mutation. Feeds Lexeme.immutable in the future pipeline; tests hand-set the flag. Class-level rules cannot be a lexeme list and are recorded as classes.

lexemes.why: fixed mutation of doe (King App A)
King §12b ('sometimes seen with AM, however' — CONTESTED)
King §12b, usually, in tu allan / tu hwnt — CONTESTED (immutable-variation)
very short native g- word: ar gro'r afon (King §12e)

classes.why: personal names do not mutate in the modern language: i Dafydd, i Pedr (King §12d, §36)
i Birmingham; but places with special Welsh names DO mutate (i °Fanceinion), as do Welsh geographical names incl. ll-: i °Landeilo (King §12c, §§37-38); speech variable — CONTESTED (placenames)
a consonant already mutated cannot mutate again (King §5c)
